def solve(s)
    n = s.length
    
    # Count of '1's in s
    c1 = s.count('1')
    
    # --- Target 1: All zeros (0^N) ---
    # Flips needed = count of '1's
    cost_t1 = c1
    
    # --- Target 2: All ones (1^N) ---
    # Flips needed = count of '0's
    cost_t2 = n - c1
    
    # --- Target 3: Exactly one '1' (0^a 1 0^b) ---
    min_cost_t3 = n # Initialize to max possible cost
    
    # Iterate through every possible position k for the single '1'
    (0...n).each do |k|
        current_cost = 0
        
        # Cost to make S[k] = '1'
        if s[k] == '0'
            current_cost += 1
        end
        
        # Cost to make all other positions S[i] = '0'
        # This is the count of '1's elsewhere
        
        # If s[k] was '1', we save 1 flip compared to c1. 
        # If s[k] was '0', we pay 1 flip for S[k], and all c1 ones remain, so we pay c1.
        
        if s[k] == '1'
            # We keep this '1'. We must flip the remaining c1 - 1 ones to zero.
            cost_to_flip_others = c1 - 1
        else # s[k] == '0'
            # We flip this '0' to '1' (cost +1). All c1 ones must be flipped to zero.
            cost_to_flip_others = c1
        end
        
        current_cost += cost_to_flip_others
        
        min_cost_t3 = [min_cost_t3, current_cost].min
    end
    
    # The overall minimum cost is the minimum of the three possibilities
    return [cost_t1, cost_t2, min_cost_t3].min
end

def solve(s)
    n = s.length
    
    # Count of '1's in s
    c1 = s.count('1')
    
    # --- Target 1: All zeros (0^N) ---
    # Flips needed = count of '1's
    cost_t1 = c1
    
    # --- Target 2: All ones (1^N) ---
    # Flips needed = count of '0's
    cost_t2 = n - c1
    
    # --- Target 3: Exactly one '1' (0^a 1 0^b) ---
    min_cost_t3 = n # Initialize to max possible cost
    
    # Iterate through every possible position k for the single '1'
    (0...n).each do |k|
        current_cost = 0
        
        # Cost to make S[k] = '1'
        if s[k] == '0'
            current_cost += 1
        end
        
        # Cost to make all other positions S[i] = '0'
        # This is the count of '1's elsewhere
        
        # If s[k] was '1', we save 1 flip compared to c1. 
        # If s[k] was '0', we pay 1 flip for S[k], and all c1 ones remain, so we pay c1.
        
        if s[k] == '1'
            # We keep this '1'. We must flip the remaining c1 - 1 ones to zero.
            cost_to_flip_others = c1 - 1
        else # s[k] == '0'
            # We flip this '0' to '1' (cost +1). All c1 ones must be flipped to zero.
            cost_to_flip_others = c1
        end
        
        current_cost += cost_to_flip_others
        
        min_cost_t3 = [min_cost_t3, current_cost].min
    end
    
    # The overall minimum cost is the minimum of the three possibilities
    return [cost_t1, cost_t2, min_cost_t3].min
end
